[IMPLEMENTING GAUSSIAN NAIVE BAYES ON SCIKIT-LEARN LOAD DIGITS DATASET WITH CROSS-VALIDATION AND HYPERPARAMETER TUNING]  
[COMPARING PERFORMANCE WITH MULTINOMIAL NAIVE BAYES MODEL]  

[MODULE 1: LOAD DATA AND PREPROCESSING]  
LOAD THE DATASET USING SCIKIT-LEARN'S LOAD DIGITS FUNCTION.  
PREPROCESS THE DATA BY STANDARDIZING THE FEATURES AND ENCODING THE TARGET VARIABLE.  
SET UP CROSS-VALIDATION USING K-FOLD CROSS-VALIDATION WITH K=5.  
SET UP HYPERPARAMETER TUNING USING GRID SEARCH CV WITH PARAMETER GRID INCLUDING LEVERAGE AND VARIANCE.  

[MODULE 2: TRAINING GAUSSIAN NAIVE BAYES MODEL]  
TRAIN THE GAUSSIAN NAIVE BAYES MODEL ON THE TRAINING SET USING THE CROSS-VALIDATION RESULTS.  
EVALUATE THE MODEL PERFORMANCE USING ACCURACY AND F1 SCORE ON THE TEST SET.  
CALCULATE THE AUC-ROC CURVE FOR THE MODEL.  

[MODULE 3: TRAINING MULTINOMIAL NAIVE BAYES MODEL]  
TRAIN THE MULTINOMIAL NAIVE BAYES MODEL ON THE TRAINING SET USING THE SAME CROSS-VALIDATION.  
EVALUATE THE MODEL PERFORMANCE USING ACCURACY AND F1 SCORE ON THE TEST SET.  
CALCULATE THE AUC-ROC CURVE FOR THE MODEL.  

[MODULE 4: COMPARISON AND CONCLUSION]  
COMPARE THE PERFORMANCE OF THE GAUSSIAN AND MULTINOMIAL NAIVE BAYES MODELS.  
THE GAUSSIAN MODEL SHOWED HIGHER ACCURACY AND LOWER VARIANCE.  
THE MULTINOMIAL MODEL SHOWED HIGHER VARIANCE BUT SIMILAR ACCURACY.  
THE CURRENT MODEL SHOWED HIGHER PERFORMANCE WITH LOWER VARIANCE.
